summary:
Claim: Accessory canals originating in the coronal third of mandibular molars are most frequently found in the distal root.  
Evidence: - Most studies report accessory canals beginning in the coronal third and extending toward the apex, primarily within the distal root. - Literature (e.g., Hahn et al.) highlights a high prevalence in the distobuccal region of mandibular first molars.  
Answer: A
